entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: Region in Africa where participatory extension approaches are being used to improve technology adoption.

entities.name: PARTICIPATORY APPROACHES
entities.description: Agricultural extension methods involving active farmer participation to improve technology adoption.

entities.name: TECHNOLOGY ADOPTION
entities.description: The process of farmers accepting and using new agricultural technologies.

entities.name: LOW-RAINFALL AREAS
entities.description: Environmental regions with limited precipitation where technology adoption is traditionally slow.

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output per unit area, which has improved through participatory approaches.

entities.name: FARM INCOMES
entities.description: The financial earnings of smallholder farmers, which have improved through participatory approaches.

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to sufficient food, which has improved through participatory approaches.

entities.name: ZIMBABWE
entities.description: Country where Farmer Field Schools were pilot-tested for delivering extension messages.

entities.name: FARMER FIELD SCHOOLS
entities.description: A participatory extension approach focusing on experimentation and collective learning-by-doing.

entities.name: SOIL AND WATER MANAGEMENT TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Agricultural technologies designed to improve the management of soil and water resources.

entities.name: DROUGHT-PRONE AREAS
entities.description: Regions susceptible to drought where soil and water management technologies are implemented.

entities.name: MASTER FARMER
entities.description: A traditional extension approach focusing on the training of lead farmers.

entities.name: PARTICIPATORY EXTENSION
entities.description: Community-based extension approaches used for technology delivery.
